title: Missing dev-bridge input injection — visual game features unverifiable via automation
summary: The player-input-system listens on window keydown events and filters out untrusted (injected) events. Playwright page.keyboard.press dispatches trusted events but only when the page's DOM focus chain is intact; the HUD overlay intercepts pointer events (canvas click fails), and keyboard events sent after the 'Press SPACE to start' modal disappears do not register as bomb placements. Attempted approaches: Playwright keyboard.press, canvas.dispatchEvent, window.dispatchEvent — all fail to trigger in-game actions after initial spawn. As a result, S90 features KABOOM-BOMB-FUSE-WIGGLE, KABOOM-DEATH-FALL, KABOOM-FOOTSTEP-TICK, and KABOOM-MINIMAP-DANGER-OVERLAY (the danger overlay requires a live bomb) could not be verified visually. Unit tests for all four pass (19 tests total). Request: add POST /__agf/input/action { entity, action } or POST /__agf/input/inject-key { code } to the dev bridge so the QA agent can trigger game actions without relying on DOM focus.
repro: Press SPACE via page.keyboard.press to spawn the player — succeeds (player appears).
Press SPACE again to place a bomb — no bomb is created; snapshot shows 0 BombFuse entities.
Attempt page.evaluate(() => window.dispatchEvent(new KeyboardEvent('keydown', {code:'Space', key:' ', bubbles:true}))) — also ignored by the input system.
Attempt page.evaluate(() => document.querySelector('canvas').dispatchEvent(...)) — fails because HUD div intercepts pointer events and canvas is never focused.
expected: A POST /__agf/input/action endpoint (or equivalent) lets QA trigger player actions programmatically to verify visual features like bomb-wiggle, death-fall animation, and minimap danger overlay without needing DOM focus.
actual: No such endpoint exists. Visual verification of any game feature requiring keyboard interaction is blocked when running from an automated session.
